visible_observations: A lean rat- or goblin-like humanoid lunges forward in a low asymmetric crouch, with long pointed ears, one visible amber eye, patchy fur, scarred skin, bare clawed feet, and dark ragged wraps.
One disproportionately long forearm reaches toward the viewer and ends in four extended fingers with translucent amber claw tips; long thin tails or tendrils curve behind the body.
The foreshortened reaching hand, low loaded legs, and trailing tail lines give the figure immediate directional motion.
defects: The sideways eye and curled smile read as sly amusement or mischievous invitation more strongly than immediate hostility.
The glossy translucent claw tips look like attached glass capsules and materially separate from the dry scarred skin and cloth.
The extreme forearm length and repeated extended fingers dominate the image so strongly that the shoulder-to-hand anatomy appears unstable.
The dark scarf, sleeveless rags, cropped trousers, and bindings supply a familiar generic scavenger or rogue costume beneath the unusual hand.
uncertainties: The image does not resolve whether the two long rear appendages are separate tails, a split tail, or another anatomy.
The claw-tip contents and their relationship to the character's body are not visually explained.
The hidden rear arm prevents confirmation of whether the asymmetrical anatomy is intentional or perspective-driven.

visible_observations: Three black candidates show an upright running thief carrying an oversized sack, a long-tailed ratlike figure lunging horizontally with a multi-pronged reaching hand, and a crouched figure almost entirely covered by a triangular cloak.
The candidates differ substantially in posture, prop emphasis, body plan, and exposed anatomy.
defects: Only the center candidate exposes the ratlike head, tail, and elongated claw hand that define the selected full-body design.
The left candidate reads as a conventional bag-carrying thief, while the right candidate reads as a cloak triangle with small hands and feet.
The center silhouette has strong motion but merges the torso, bent legs, rear hand, and lower garment into a dense central mass.
uncertainties: The image contains no A, B, C labels or explicit selection marker.
The three candidates vary enough in prop and body presentation that it is unclear which identity elements were intended to remain constant.

visible_observations: The head tilts forward and sideways, with one amber eye fixed toward the viewer, one side hidden by black hair, a raised or relaxed brow, and a crooked closed-mouth smile.
Long pierced ears, a pointed nose, scar lines, mottled skin, sparse fur, and facial asymmetry make the head visually specific.
defects: The crooked smile and open visible eye produce a playful, crafty, or impish read that can soften the predatory body language.
The expression shows little fear, strain, hunger, or conflict; its strongest unaided signal is self-satisfied mischief.
Hair fully hides one eye, limiting evidence of bilateral eye tension and target focus.
uncertainties: Unaided viewing cannot determine whether the smile is predatory anticipation, social charm, mockery, or harmless amusement.
The face crop does not include the oversized reaching hand, so agreement between facial intent and the attack-like gesture remains uncertain.

visible_observations: At native size, the pointed ear, pale face, low forward lean, narrow legs, oversized reaching hand, and four bright amber claw tips remain visible.
The bright fingertips form the clearest focal accent against the otherwise dark figure and background.
defects: Facial scars, fur patches, smile nuance, clothing layers, and tail anatomy collapse at this size.
The torso, rear arm, and dark garments merge into one central mass.
The oversized glowing-looking fingertips become more prominent than the character's face or species identity and may read as a generic claw effect.
uncertainties: No neighboring unit icons, gameplay background, selection frame, or motion state are shown, so in-context recognition and apparent threat cannot be determined.
